danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites positioned in platformer level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threats that can be defeated by jumping on them, tail strikes, or thrown nuts; contact without sufficient defeat causes health loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Level design obstacles and environmental dangers (implicit in 'hazards' gameplay mention)
danger_signals.meaning: Indestructible or unavoidable level elements that cause health loss on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Yeti character sprite, likely large/imposing visual presentation distinct from regular enemies
danger_signals.meaning: World-threatening antagonist attempting to freeze the world; requires defeat to progress narrative; cannot be defeated by standard enemy-kill mechanics

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite flashing during invulnerability frames after damage
danger_signals.meaning: Current vulnerability state and recovery window after taking damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable coin sprites distributed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe pickups that increase score and determine completion bonus; hidden coins reward exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Health recovery pickups hidden throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe items that restore health units; hidden placement encourages cautious exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: 1-Up or life container sprites hidden in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe collectibles that extend player longevity and reduce failure consequences

safety_signals.visual_form: Nut sprites collected and displayed as inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Safe resource acquisition that enables safe-distance threat elimination; non-depleting in levels

safety_signals.visual_form: Password system display at level transitions (post-SNES versions)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression markers allowing players to resume from reached levels without restart penalty

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in foreground sprite layer; player-controllable movement patterns distinguish from static world elements

faction_indicators.color_identity: White/Ice-themed (implied by yeti design and ice world takeover theme)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Single boss entity with unique sprite set; visually distinct from standard enemies through scale and animation complexity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with ice-themed level design; magic-induced environmental transformation (frozen world state)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse enemy sprite designs across six themed levels
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct enemy types per level, each with unique sprite work and behavior patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Positioned within level-specific environmental contexts; themed to match level aesthetics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six themed levels with natural or neutral environmental aesthetics; vegetation, water, cave structures visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patrol patterns; environmental hazards stationary; level geometry stable and traversable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive environmental icing/freezing implied through level-specific ice-themed aesthetics; water stages present as environmental storytelling of frozen world impacts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter escalation; Mr. Blizzard uses magic powers to transform environment; player must navigate increasingly hazardous frozen terrain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Coin counter and score display; completion bonus calculation visible at level end; password display in GBC/GBA versions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy populations remain constant until level reset; checkpoints enable non-linear resume capability

environmental_storytelling.example: Six distinct themed levels communicate narrative progression toward Mr. Blizzard; level aesthetics shift from natural/neutral environments toward ice-world consequences of yeti antagonist's magic

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type placement in environmental context
environmental_storytelling.example: Enemies themed to match level aesthetics (water level enemies, cave enemies, ice realm enemies) communicate world-specific threats and environmental character
Water stage removal from original release, later restored as bonus content in GBA version, suggests iteration on world-building; water mechanics communicate environmental hazard progression
Mr. Blizzard as singular boss entity distinct from standard enemies communicates unique narrative threat status; yeti design directly visualizes world-freezing threat
Placement of coins, health, and lives rewards exploration of level geometry; communicates safe resource availability in dangerous environments

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can run, jump, swim in designated levels; responds to controller input with immediate animation state changes
Sprite flashes during invulnerability frames; temporary invulnerability prevents re-damage during recovery window; movement continues uninterrupted
Predictable movement patterns within level geometry; can be jumped upon, tail-struck, or nut-struck for defeat
Most enemies destroyed with single strike (visual: disappearance/death animation); removed from level population
Complex animation cycles; uses magic powers to transform environment; cannot be defeated by standard enemy-kill mechanics; requires boss-specific strategy
Defeat stops world-freezing threat; end-goal narrative condition achieved; game conclusion triggered
Stationary sprite display in level geometry; visual distinction by item type (coin sparkle, health glow, life icon)
Removal from level; instantaneous pickup; inventory/score/health counter increment

notes: Mr. Nutz uses straightforward visual communication typical of early-1990s platformers: protagonist colored distinctly (red squirrel), antagonist visually imposing (yeti), and world-state communicated through level theming rather than dynamic environmental transformation. The game lacks documented faction complexity or political narrative elements; conflict is straightforward good (player) vs evil (yeti antagonist). Visual manifestation relies on sprite design clarity, level aesthetic theming, and simple behavioral animation cycles. The water stage's inclusion/exclusion across versions suggests environmental storytelling iteration. Safety vs danger signals communicated through sprite type (enemy vs collectible), animation state (flashing = vulnerable), and level geometry context (hazard placement).
